author: Patrick Stox
title: Does Being Mentioned on Highly Linked Pages Influence AI Mentions?
content:
Does being mentioned on highly cited web pages lead to more mentions in AI assistants? This research looked at highly cited web pages as those with more Referring Domains (RD) - prominent pages on the web that other websites have deemed worthy of linking to.

Having your brand present on these pages means you likely have more credibility and are a known brand.

Methodology:
The research examined the top 50 websites mentioned in Ahrefs Brand Radar for Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, and Perplexity. This was across approximately 76.7 million AI Overviews, 957,000 ChatGPT prompts, and 953,500 Perplexity prompts for June 2025.

Key findings:
A correlation does not equal causation disclaimer applies, especially here.

Spearman rank correlations for mentions on highly cited web pages versus AI visibility across the top 50 domains:
- Google AI Overviews: 0.70 correlation (Strong)
- ChatGPT: 0.12 correlation (Very weak)
- Perplexity: 0.40 correlation (Moderate)

Interpretation:
It shouldn't come as a surprise that prominent brands are mentioned on pages that website owners deemed worthy enough to link to. Google favors brands and Perplexity seems to show some favoritism as well.

What's surprising is the weakness of ChatGPT here. The weak correlation suggests that ChatGPT leans heavily on partnerships for top citation sources rather than being influenced by mentions on highly linked pages.

Differences across AI systems:
- Google AI Overviews shows a strong correlation, suggesting that being mentioned on highly linked pages significantly influences AI mentions.
- Perplexity shows a moderate correlation, indicating some influence from mentions on highly linked pages.
- ChatGPT shows very weak correlation, suggesting that mentions on highly linked pages have minimal influence on its citation patterns.

Future research:
It will be interesting to see how this changes when the research is run for a larger number of websites.

Brand visibility:
Brand Radar is described as more than just another LLM visibility monitor. It tracks a large amount of queries across all AI systems. Users can query for any product, service, or brand and compare versus competitors. Brand Radar provides web visibility index to see how brands are talked about online and search demand index to see how popular they are in searches.
